description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with three stages: build, test, and publish. In the build stage add a build-dockers job that uses the docker:18.01.0 image with the docker:dind service, runs only on master and tags, and executes the exact commands: docker version, docker build -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest ., docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN $CI_REGISTRY, docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest, then checks if CI_BUILD_TAG is set ("[[ -z $CI_BUILD_TAG ]] && exit 0"), tags the image with $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_TAG and pushes it. In the test stage add four jobs: test-dockers using the registry.gitlab.com/sasol/gitlab-ci-git-tools:latest image, test-dockers-install_script using spotify/alpine:latest, test-wrong-sed-version using sasol/shathel:0.3.0 with entrypoint [/bin/bash,-c], and test-dockers-push-no-change also using the git-tools image; all run only on master and tags and run the listed scripts (e.g., echo "tests here", git-edit, touch $CI_COMMIT_SHA, git-commit, git-push origin testbranch, rm $CI_COMMIT_SHA, git-pull origin testbranch, ls $CI_COMMIT_SHA, plus the curl command to download and execute get-gitlab-ci-git-tools.sh in the install_script and wrong-sed-version jobs). In the publish stage add a publish-dockers job that runs only on tags, uses docker:18.01.0 with docker:dind, and runs docker build -t $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest ., docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN $CI_REGISTRY, docker tag $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_TAG, and docker push $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$CI_BUILD_TAG.
